steps.title: Find the value of $x + y$
steps.content: Then $x+y = 5+8 = 13$. That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that $4x=20$ and $-3x+y=-7$ is a system of equations with a solution $\left(x,y\right)$. Adding the second equation in the given system to the first equation yields $4x+(-3x+y)=20+\left(-7\right)$, which is equivalent to $x+y=13$. Thus, the value of $x+y$ is $13$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This represents the value of $-2\left(x+y\right)-1$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This represents the value of $-\left(x+y\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This represents the value of $2\left(x+y\right)+1$.
